schemeContent.detailedDescription_md: The National Energy Conservation Award under the Ministry of Power, Government of India, recognizes organizations for their outstanding efforts in energy conservation and energy efficiency.A total of 65 awards are given under different categories, including Tier-I and Tier-II. The award ceremony is held every year on 14th December on the occasion of National Energy Conservation Day. **Objective** a) Scope: The scope of the Awards is to cover sectors(Industries, Building, Transport, Institutions, Equipments) which have a share of around 56% of India’s total Electricity consumption(Energy Statistics 2022, MOSPI). b) Purpose: The purpose of the awardis to recognize/reward organizations for the exemplary efforts on Energy Conservation and Energy Efficiency. It can be categorized into National Award(Tier-I/Tier-II) category.
schemeContent.exclusions_md: **However, the following organizations cannot apply for the Award:** a. Organizations having legal issues, blacklisted, or defaulters in any of the government schemes. b. Defaulters/repeated defaulters under the Perform Achieve and Trade scheme of the Bureau of Energy Efficiency, Ministry of Power.
schemeContent.briefDescription: The scheme aims to recognize and reward organizations for their exemplary efforts in energy conservation and energy efficiency.

children.text: The National Energy Conservation Award under the Ministry of Power, Government of India, recognizes organizations for their outstanding efforts in energy conservation and energy efficiency.A total of 65 awards are given under different categories, including Tier-I and Tier-II. The award ceremony is held every year on 14th December on the occasion of National Energy Conservation Day.

a) Scope: The scope of the Awards is to cover sectors(Industries, Building, Transport, Institutions, Equipments) which have a share of around 56% of India’s total Electricity consumption(Energy Statistics 2022, MOSPI).

b) Purpose: The purpose of the awardis to recognize/reward organizations for the exemplary efforts on Energy Conservation and Energy Efficiency. It can be categorized into National Award(Tier-I/Tier-II) category.
However, the following organizations cannot apply for the Award:
a. Organizations having legal issues, blacklisted, or defaulters in any of the government schemes.
b. Defaulters/repeated defaulters under the Perform Achieve and Trade scheme of the Bureau of Energy Efficiency, Ministry of Power.

applicationProcess.process_md: **Registration:** **Step 1:** Visit the o[fficial website](https://awards.gov.in/) and click on the[ Registration button.](https://awards.gov.in/Registration/SignUp) **Step 2:** Select your category: Individual or Organization. **Step 3:** Fill in the required details: Individual: Name, Date of Birth, Nominator Type, Mobile, Email, Aadhaar Number. Organization: Type, Authorized Person Name, Date of Birth, Organization Description, Designation, Mobile, Email, Aadhaar Number. **Step 4:** Click Submit to complete registration. **Apply:** **Step 1:** [Log in](https://awards.gov.in/Login) to the portal using your registered credentials. **Step 2:** Select the scheme from the list of awards and click "[Nominate / Apply Now".](https://awards.gov.in/Home/AwardLibrary) **Step 3:** Fill in all mandatory fields in the online application form. **Step 4:** Click Submit to complete your application.

eligibilityCriteria.eligibilityDescription_md: All organizations are eligible to apply free of cost for the National Energy Conservation Award subject to the condition that their sector should be eligible for Award for a particular year.

children.text: All organizations are eligible to apply free of cost for the National Energy Conservation Award subject to the condition that their sector should be eligible for Award for a particular year.
